meaning: adj.不赞成的,恳求的,反对的
examples: Accordingly he is not Fried besides oneself, deprecatory also employee is Fried.
The steward stands facing him at the other side of the table, if so deprecatory a stance as his can be called standing.
For a gracious nobleman in Renaissance Urbino, that meant being able to finish dancing the most elaborate saltarello with a double hop and a self-deprecatory shrug.
Regrettably, most people cannot: Folks reflexively react to errors by either externalizing blame or, more problematically, hitting themselves with self-deprecatory, ad hominem invective.
His manifest brilliance and energy remain, along with the bravodo, but these days, the weapon he wields with visitors is likely to be self-deprecatory humor, not intimidation.
